audit_items.proof_trace: deleteEntry() frees TransliteratorEntry including its compoundFilter member (destructor L483: delete compoundFilter), but instantiateEntry() at L1266 (ALIAS) and L1299 (COMPOUND_RBT) passes entry->compoundFilter as a raw non-owning pointer to TransliteratorAlias, which stores it without copying. In createBasicInstance() (translit.cpp), registryMutex is released after get() (line 983) but before alias->create() (line 1019); a concurrent Transliterator::unregister() call within that window triggers deleteEntry() on the same entry, freeing compoundFilter while the alias still holds a dangling pointer. The source comment at lines 30-36 of transreg.cpp explicitly notes that BoundsChecker reported dangling pointer errors with these entry objects, corroborating the race.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Two threads concurrently operate on the same COMPOUND_RBT transliterator ID: Thread A calls Transliterator::createBasicInstance(), obtains a TransliteratorAlias holding a raw compoundFilter pointer from registry->get(), releases registryMutex, and begins alias->create() which calls compoundFilter->clone(); simultaneously, Thread B calls Transliterator::unregister() which acquires the mutex, calls registry->remove(), and triggers deleteEntry() that frees the entry including compoundFilter — leaving Thread A's alias with a dangling pointer and producing a use-after-free in compoundFilter->clone().
